<<vegetable>> <<actor>> <<bottom>> <<pack>> <<sick>> <<cost>>

The device is modeled as a three-dimensional shape that can be described by the following parametric equations:

<<x(u, v)>> <<= a sin(u) cos(v)>>  
<<y(u, v)>> <<= a sin(u) sin(v)>>  
<<z(u, v)>> <<= b cos(u)>>  

where $ 0 \leq u \leq \pi $ and $ 0 \leq v < 2\pi $. Given that the surface area of this shape must be exactly 1000 square units to ensure optimal heat dissipation, find the values of $ a $ and $ b $ that satisfy this condition.

<<The surface area>> <<of this shape>> <<is given by the surface integral>> <<of the function>> <<sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2)>> <<over the domain>> <<of u and v>>.  

<<The surface integral>> <<can be evaluated>> <<using the parametric equations>> <<and the surface area>> <<formula>> <<for parametric surfaces>> <<which is>> <<the double integral>> <<of the magnitude>> <<of the cross product>> <<of the partial derivatives>> <<of x and y with respect to u and v>> <<over the domain>> <<of u and v>>.  

<<The cross product>> <<of the partial derivatives>> <<is>> <<given by>> <<the determinant>> <<of the matrix>> <<with columns>> <<the partial derivatives of x with respect to u and v>> <<and the partial derivatives of y with respect to u and v>>.  

<<The magnitude>> <<of the cross product>> <<is>> <<the square root>> <<of the sum of the squares>> <<of the components>> <<of the cross product>> <<which gives the surface area>> <<of the shape>> <<as a function>> <<of a and b>>.  

<<The surface area>> <<is given by>> <<the double integral>> <<of the magnitude>> <<of the cross product>> <<of the partial derivatives>> <<of x and y with respect to u and v>> <<over the domain>> <<of u and v>>.
